evals.name: vague-test-rn-app
evals.prompt: Test my React Native app on Android and tell me if the login screen renders correctly after my change.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs preflight (check-devices.sh, node + package manager), reads references/react-native.md for the run command (`npx react-native run-android`), launches it via launch.sh (Metro starts in the same process group), waits for BUILD SUCCESSFUL + Metro 'Bundling 100%' + first frame with no red-box, navigates to the login screen, screenshots via snap.sh, reads Metro/logcat (ReactNative/ReactNativeJS), and reports a verdict.

evals.name: expo-app
evals.prompt: Run my Expo app on the emulator and check the new profile tab.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes Expo (app.json/app.config, expo dependency), uses `npx expo run:android` (or a dev client) per references/react-native.md rather than assuming bare RN, finds the package via android.package, waits for the bundle + first frame, navigates to the profile tab, screenshots, and verdicts. Uses local `npx expo`, not the deprecated global expo-cli.

evals.name: red-box-detection
evals.prompt: The app looks broken on Android after my edit — can you see what's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Agent launches the app, screenshots, and detects a React Native red-box error screen in the PNG, then cross-references the Metro/logcat output (ReactNativeJS stack, Hermes) to quote the actual JS error and name the likely offending file. Distinguishes a real red box from benign logcat noise.
